items.title: Iran's supreme leader Khamenei killed in Israeli airstrike on Tehran compound

content.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader since 1989, was killed in an Israeli airstrike on his Tehran compound, according to four Israeli security officials. US officials said they did not immediately have confirmation.

items.title: US and Israel launch major attack on Iran — Operation Shield of Judah targets nuclear facilities

content.abstract: The US and Israel launched a coordinated major military attack on Iran targeting nuclear and military facilities across multiple cities. Roughly 200 Israeli fighter jets struck 500+ targets including aerial defense systems and missile launchers simultaneously.

items.title: Multiple Arab states hosting US assets targeted in Iran retaliation

content.abstract: Iran launched missile attacks across Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Bahrain and Kuwait in retaliation for joint Israel-US strikes, targeting US military bases. This significantly expands the conflict beyond Israel-Iran bilateral dimension.

items.title: Airspace closed, airlines halt flights as US-Israel attack Iran

content.abstract: At least 14,000 departure and arrival flights affected. Eight states closed airspace: Iran, Israel, Iraq, Jordan, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait and UAE. Major disruption to global aviation routes.

items.title: Trump confirms Khamenei death reports — oil surges past $120, markets in turmoil

content.abstract: Trump told NBC News he believes reports of Khamenei's death are 'correct'. Global markets in severe turmoil — oil prices surge past $120/barrel. European and Asian markets plunge. Gold hits record high.

items.title: Daily Report: The Second Iran War — February 28, 2026

content.abstract: Comprehensive military analysis of the first day of the Second Iran War. More than 200 killed across Iran, 747 injured, strikes hit 24 of Iran's 31 provinces. Israel deployed approximately 200 fighter jets.

items.title: Taiwan arms sale approved by US Congress delayed as Trump plans Beijing visit

content.abstract: Trump administration delayed announcing billions in Taiwan arms sales including air-defense missiles to avoid upsetting Xi Jinping ahead of Trump's planned Beijing visit in April. Package was approved by bipartisan congressional leadership.

items.title: China urges vigilance as Japan signals defense policy shift

content.abstract: China urged international community to remain vigilant against neo-militarism following Japanese PM Takaichi's remarks accusing China of intensifying efforts to alter the status quo through force in East China Sea, South China Sea, and areas around Japan.

items.title: China ousts nine military lawmakers as Xi's defense purge widens

content.abstract: China ousted nine military lawmakers as part of Xi Jinping's expanding defense purge. This follows Xi's move to probe his top general and one-time ally Zhang Youxia, representing the widest military anti-corruption campaign in decades.

items.title: Bank of Korea holds rate at 2.5% — extends 9-month pause amid recovery confidence

content.abstract: Bank of Korea held benchmark rate at 2.5%, extending nine-month pause. Monetary policymakers signaled greater confidence in economic recovery. Decision made before Iran crisis fully materialized in Korean time.

items.title: Korea exports jump 44.4% YoY in early February — semiconductor demand surge

content.abstract: South Korea's exports jumped 44.4% year-over-year in the first 10 days of February, driven by strong semiconductor demand. Outbound shipments reached $21.39 billion.

items.title: EU Fit for 90 package — binding 90% emissions reduction by 2040

content.abstract: EU prepares 'Fit for 90' package — new set of laws to achieve binding 90% net reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2040. Expected in second half of 2026. Reframing transition as 'Energy Sovereignty' to counter populist resistance.

items.title: European energy crisis deepens — high electricity prices threaten green transition

content.abstract: European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change warned EU is inadequately prepared for accelerating climate impacts. Called for urgent investment increases to protect from floods, wildfires, and severe heat waves. Iran conflict exacerbates energy price pressures.

items.title: TSMC posts record monthly revenue — $12.7B in January, up 37% YoY on AI chip demand

content.abstract: TSMC reported record monthly revenue of NT$401.26 billion ($12.7 billion) in January, up 37% YoY and 20% MoM. Driven by AI chip demand. Stock hit record high. Industry tracking toward approximately $1 trillion annual semiconductor sales in 2026.

items.title: Semiconductor industry outlook 2026 — HBM and advanced packaging as key bottlenecks

content.abstract: Semiconductor industry forecasts 12-15% revenue growth in 2026 driven by AI. True constraints identified as HBM and advanced packaging throughput. Samsung advanced HBM4 DRAM mass production. Industry tracking toward $1T annual sales.

items.title: 조선일보: 이란 전쟁 발발 — 한국 기업 중동 사업 비상

content.abstract: 이란 전쟁 발발로 한국 기업의 중동 사업 전반에 비상이 걸렸다. 건설, 방산, 에너지 분야 기업들이 긴급 대책 회의를 소집. UAE 650억 달러 방산 딜 이행에도 영향 가능성.

items.title: 매일경제: 반도체 수출 호조에도 이란 리스크 — 1분기 경기 불확실성 고조

content.abstract: 2월 초 반도체 수출이 44.4% 증가하는 호조세에도 불구하고, 이란 전쟁 발발로 1분기 경기 불확실성이 급격히 고조. 유가 상승과 환율 변동이 기업 수익성에 직접 영향.

items.title: 한국경제: 대법관 증원 논란 — 기업법 환경 변화 우려

content.abstract: 대법관 14명에서 26명으로 증원 법안 통과에 기업계가 우려를 표명. 노동법, 환경규제, 기업지배구조 관련 판결 변화 가능성에 대한 법적 불확실성 증가.

items.title: 한겨레: 파키스탄-아프간 전쟁 선언 — 이슬람 세계 다전선 위기

content.abstract: 파키스탄의 아프간 공습과 전쟁 선언으로 이슬람 세계에 다전선 위기가 조성. 이란 전쟁과 결합하여 글로벌 안보 구조에 전례 없는 스트레스. 한반도 안보에도 간접 영향.

items.title: 블로터: 에이전틱 AI 도입 가속 — 기업 59.3% 최우선 투자 영역으로

content.abstract: 에이전틱 AI가 2026년 기업 IT 최우선 투자 영역으로 부상. 조사 대상 기업의 59.3%가 생성형 AI 투자를 최우선시. SK텔레콤 A.X K1, 삼성 갤럭시 AI 등 주요 기업 경쟁 심화.

items.title: 전자신문: HBM4·HBF 경쟁 격화 — 삼성-SK 메모리 패권 재편

content.abstract: HBM4 양산과 HBF 표준화 컨소시엄 출범으로 메모리 반도체 경쟁이 새 국면에 진입. 삼성의 HBM4 양산 가속과 SK하이닉스의 HBF 선도 전략이 대조. 반도체 산업 약 $1조 규모 전망.

items.title: China masking drone flights in potential Taiwan rehearsal — SCMP analysis

content.abstract: Military analysts say recent Chinese drone flights represent a step-change in China's gray-zone tactics in the contested South China Sea. Flights are masking potential Taiwan invasion rehearsals. Japan-US-Philippines conducted multilateral maritime exercises Feb 20-26.
